Now the Artemis capsule has left to the moon we have 8 days to get everyone on earth an ape costume for the most epic prank ever.

NASA's Artemis II crew say they received a terrifying warning message as their spacecraft passed the 'point of no return' on its trip to the moon. The Orion capsule, containing astronauts Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen, is finally on its way to the moon after carrying out a 'translunar injection burn'. This engine burn is a key manoeuvre that nudges the spacecraft out of Earth's orbit and on its trajectory towards our lunar neighbour.

Can’t quite get my head around ‘frozen Greek feta pasta salad’. Cucumber not nice when defrosted.

Farmfoods has more supermarkets than Waitrose, a yearly turnover of more than one billion pounds and is the UK's second-biggest frozen food retailer behind Iceland - and yet, until recently, many would be hard-pressed to name it. But now, the budget store - which has 340 locations across the UK - has become an unlikely haven for middle-class mothers in pursuit of a bargain amid the cost-of-living crisis. These days, a whole host of British shoppers - and social media 'mumfluencers' to boot - are now supplementing their posh food hauls from M&S and Waitrose with deals from Farmfoods. Unlike its more expensive competitors, the store doesn't offer mums a cafe for a pastry and coffee, has no shop windows for browsing, doesn't subscribe to a loyalty scheme and has a limited advertising budget when compared to its rivals. Yet a quick scroll on the Scottish brand's Instagram, or a look on X, showcases its growing popularity, especially with parents looking for a bargain on their favourite middle-class items, like Greek Feta Pasta salads (pictured left) and salmon fillets (pictured centre).
  • 267 comments

A nine-year-old girl has died and two children have been injured following a suspected drug driving car crash. Two men aged 22 and 23 were arrested after officers attended the scene in Icknield Street in the Hockley area of Birmingham at around 11pm. West Midlands Police said the pair, from a second vehicle, were held on suspicion of causing death by dangerous driving. Photos taken in the aftermath of the crash show the remains of two mangled cars on the roadside as officers carry out their inspections. The nine-year-old, a passenger in one of two cars involved in the collision, was taken to hospital with serious injuries but died a short while later.

Your article is correct to a point but misses out the fact that if the persons AHI ( Apnea-Hypopnea Index, the number of times they stop breathing in an hour) is over 30 it is life threatening as the O2 level drops to the point of hypoxia which occurs when O2 is under about 85% then it causes seizures. How do I know? I have exactly this condition that occurred suddenly & I have had 7 incidents in 2 years! Stay safe and hope the research gets answers.

Dementia is a horrible disease and is a tragic way to slowly die.

When most people think of dementia, a specific image comes to mind. However, there are several subtypes of the disease that fly under the radar and don't cause hallmark memory loss off the bat.
  • 104 comments
  • 41 shares Four dementia types people don't know exist... and signs to watch for

My wife found out purely by chance following a slightly elevated LFT in a non connected blood test was then subsequently told the mass in the pancreas was inoperable and that it had also spread to the liver and lungs, she then started Cheamo which can be really hard but she was so strong through it, early results were extremely positive on the kidney and lungs with the mass stable in the pancreas and still had absolutely no symptoms of the disease unfortunately after 15 months she found out that in an extremely rare mutation it had spread to the brain and sadly passed away within a few weeks. Hindsight is a wonderful and also terrible thing and the NHS Oncology team's do a fantastic job but if you're financially able to once diagnosed please go privately for a full body MRI scan to get a full picture of the illness. And one last thing as my wife did stay absolutely positive throughout.

The Princess of Wales boasts an impressive handbag collection, with a number of British brands firmly in her fashion rotation. One of her favourites is DeMellier, celebrated for its minimalist aesthetic and timeless designs that strike the perfect balance between practicality and elegance. While Kate already owns two styles from the label, she recently introduced a new addition to her wardrobe: the Small Hudson in Mocha Suede, which she carried during a visit to the Anna Freud Centre. Unsurprisingly, royal watchers were quick to swoon over the chic arm candy. And it's easy to see why - the compact design is ideal for daily essentials, while the sumptuous suede finish and gold-plated hardware transition seamlessly from day to evening.

  • Sorry, but this just doesn't work. Susan represented the unseen, unheard, average if not totally introverted village lady....with a huge hidden talent. The fact that she had no "style" is what made that talent such a surprise. It was part of her persona, that she made no attempt to have any style, it was all about her voice. She doesn't have the inherent social skills to be an "influencer." She also had to be dressed, styled, clothed and managed in order to get past her social limitations and drab look. Because of that, none of these items come off as genuine, but rather as a curated construct from a design team, not her. MAYBE if this was produced quickly in 2009 right after she exploded onto the scene, but even then, she's not really marketable. She would never have come up with any of this on her own. Just a rather strange and weirdly timed launch.

In Japan there are no bins on the street, people are expected to take their rubbish/ litter home with them. In any other country this would cause huge problems, however the Japanese accept this and will carry their litter home & probably have the most clean streets in the world.

From a vacuum sealers, to egg box holders and even food disinfectant machines - there is seemingly no shortage of niche products to help keep your home clean and organised amid the modern daily stresses. The video, posted to TikTok, has amassed more than 8,000 likes and shows a woman's average cleaning routine, but with a few rather remarkable twists. Gone are the days of simply using a mop and a hoover during your Sunday reset. Now, a wave of innovative yet unusual machines have taken over the home in a global movement dubbed 'smart living'.
  • 205 comments
  • 1 video
  • 50 shares Social media users stunned by Japanese cleaning gadgets

Fuerteventua is nothing like the Carribean. Who are these people peddling tripe?

Rather than spending up to £1,500 on a week in Barbados or Jamaica, travel experts suggest a £396 package trip to a Spanish island that's only four hours away from the UK.

  • share The Spanish island that feels like the Caribbean - but is 68% cheaper

I’m joining the Cotswold Set. I’m printing my own teeshirt that reads “I am a member of the Cotswold Set”. After that I plan to wave a flag outside the Co-op in Chipping Norton to further remind folks of my elevated status from the Jaywick On Sea Set. Bonjour.

A Grade II-listed four bedroom house in the Cotswolds is on the market for £995,000. The Cotswolds has become a popular destination for the rich and famous, with a growing number of celebrities opting to buy property in the idyllic district. The townhouse is located in Stow-on-the-Wold and dates back to the early nineteenth century.
  • 14 comments
  • share Join the Cotswolds set with this Grade II-listed Stow-in-the-Wold home

  • It's bad luck in a way for Gatfiso as his record was not too bad. However, it is right. A coach like Gattuso is wrong for Italy. In the past, such a coach would never have been considered. It was a desperate act. His style and methods are Jurassic in approach. If Italy are going to lose they must do so with new younger players in a way that progresses the attitude to the game, its rules and officials. This will take a vision and a strategy, time and patience before a dangerous and winning side emerges.
